name: Lutheran Social Services Of Southern California- San Bernardino Branch Care Center
description_short: Faith-based nonprofit providing emergency assistance, housing support, food services, and social programs to vulnerable individuals and families across Southern California.
description_long:
Lutheran Social Services of Southern California (LSSSC) is a faith-based nonprofit organization serving vulnerable populations across Southern California. The organization is ignited by faith and operates with the mission to embrace, equip, and empower individuals, families, and communities toward self-sufficiency. In 2024, LSSSC served over 60,000 Southern Californians in need across multiple service categories.

LSSC offers a comprehensive array of free and low-cost services including emergency food assistance (42,383 meals served in 2024), housing support for 1,115 individuals experiencing housing insecurity, mental health services to 4,026 people, and specialized programs serving seniors, people with developmental disabilities, and students. Services span food pantries, clothing closets, community meal programs, men's homeless shelter (30/60/90-day case-managed program), housing referrals, rental assistance vouchers, transportation assistance, dental and vision care vouchers, eviction prevention counseling, and probation counseling. The organization operates 40 specialized programs across multiple Southern California locations including San Bernardino, Long Beach, Riverside County, San Diego, Orange County, and Ventura County.

LSSC distinguishes itself through its strong faith-based mission combined with data-driven community impact metrics. The organization has achieved a 100% graduation rate through its high school and college prep courses and maintains 40 specialized programs designed to move clients toward independence. Their community meal program is volunteer-driven, with over 80,000 meals prepared and served annually through 50+ community groups. The organization also operates specialized transitional services including grief counseling, mentorship programs, and workforce training.

As a nonprofit, LSSSC relies on donations and grants rather than generating revenue, which may affect service capacity during funding shortages. Services vary by location, requiring clients to call ahead for availability. The organization does not provide direct financial assistance or credit-related services, positioning it purely as an emergency assistance and social services provider for basic human needs.
pros: Served 42,383 people with meals and food assistance in 2024 with fresh and non-perishable options
Operates 40 specialized programs designed to equip individuals with tools for independence
Achieved 100% graduation rate through high school and college prep courses
365-day men's homeless shelter with case management and structured 30/60/90-day programs
Comprehensive services across multiple counties (San Bernardino, Riverside, Orange, Ventura, San Diego)
Faith-based holistic approach addressing emergency needs plus mental health, workforce training, and community integration
Over 80,000 volunteer-prepared meals annually through community partnerships
cons: Services vary significantly by location; many programs not available at all LSSSC branches
Must call ahead to verify service availability rather than guaranteed access
Limited housing assistance (only 1,115 people helped with housing in 2024 across entire service area)
Reliance on donations creates potential service gaps during funding shortages
No direct financial assistance or credit repair services available
best_for: Individuals and families experiencing food insecurity or hunger in Southern California
Men experiencing homelessness seeking case-managed shelter with structured transition support
Low-income individuals needing emergency housing, eviction prevention, or rental assistance
People seeking mental health services, workforce training, or community integration programs
diagnosis: Best for low-income Southern California residents needing emergency assistance with food, shelter, housing, or transportation. Primary caveat: services and availability vary significantly by location, requiring advance verification by phone, and the organization provides emergency/social services only—not financial products or credit-related assistance.
services: Food Pantry with fresh and non-perishable foods
Clothing Closet organized by season and gender
Community Meal Programs with volunteer-prepared meals
Men's Homeless Shelter (30/60/90-day case-managed program, 365 days/year)
Housing Referrals for Section 8 and subsidized housing
Motel and Rental Assistance Vouchers for crisis situations
Dental, Vision, and Prescription Assistance vouchers
Workforce Training and Student Academic Programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1329 N H St, San Bernardino, CA 92405
